name: Chat-stream HTML widgets
summary: The chat-stream and dashboard HTML widgets behind a URI. Serves one widget's JS, one importable ES module bundle (+ CSS), and server-side HTML for service views or named dashboard widgets.
description: widget:// makes chat-stream views and dashboard widgets first-class reusable UI instead of inline functions on the host dashboard. The browser source of truth is the standalone ES modules under assets/widgets/. widget://host/registry/query/list enumerates the catalogue (id, the `view` keys each service widget handles, the data shape it expects). widget://host/widget/query/get?name=table returns one widget's metadata plus its ES module source. widget://host/bundle/query/js returns helpers + every widget + two dispatchers in one module: renderServiceView(view) for live/service views and renderDashboardWidget(name, data) for named dashboard widgets such as artifact-grid, chat-message and contacts. widget://host/bundle/query/css returns the shared stylesheet. widget://host/widget/query/render renders a service view (`view=table`) or dashboard widget (`widget=artifact-grid`) to an HTML string with the Python mirror. Pairs with the scanner/camera flow (scanner-stream + scanner-status visualise camera://host scans) and artifact:// (widgets can link to artifacts without owning artifact persistence).
useCases: List every chat-stream widget and the view keys it handles.
Load the whole widget catalogue into chatStreamList from one ES module import.
Render dashboard widgets such as artifact-grid, chat-message and contacts through one dispatcher.
Fetch the shared widget stylesheet for a standalone page.
Render a table/scanner-stream view to HTML server-side for an email digest or test.
Get one widget's JS source to embed or customise.
